items.headline: AI Rally Propels S&P 500 and Nasdaq to Fresh Record Highs Amid Strong Jobs Data
items.summary: Wall Street closed higher on May 8, with both the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite achieving new record highs. This surge was primarily driven by continued investor enthusiasm for artificial intelligence (AI)-linked stocks, despite ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Positive sentiment was further bolstered by stronger-than-expected April jobs data, reinforcing expectations that the Federal Reserve will maintain current interest rates through year-end. Technology stocks were the clear leaders, while utilities lagged.

items.headline: Micron Technology
items.summary: Memory and storage firm Micron Technology surged over 15% on optimism regarding booming demand tied to AI data center expansion.

items.headline: Intel Corp.
items.summary: Intel shares rose significantly, between 10% and 19%, after reports emerged of a preliminary agreement for Intel Foundry to manufacture processors for Apple devices.

items.headline: Cloudflare Inc.
items.summary: Cloudflare tumbled 24% after the company announced job cuts and provided weaker-than-expected revenue guidance.

items.headline: The Trade Desk
items.summary: The Trade Desk fell 1.8% on soft revenue forecasts, with pre-market indications showing a larger drop of 12%.

items.headline: April Jobs Report Exceeds Expectations
items.summary: America's employers added a surprising 115,000 new jobs in April, surpassing forecasters' expectations of 65,000, despite economic pressures from the Iran war. The unemployment rate held steady at a low 4.3%. Average hourly earnings rose 0.2% month-on-month, below the 0.3% forecast, indicating a decelerating wage growth trend that may ease inflation concerns.

items.headline: Technology and Semiconductors Lead Market Gains
items.summary: The technology sector spearheaded market performance, with the S&P 500 technology index jumping 2.7%. The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index continued its strong run, extending its second-quarter gain to 55%, driven by robust demand for AI infrastructure. In contrast, the utilities sector experienced a decline of 0.9%.

items.headline: Q1 Earnings Season Surpasses Expectations
items.summary: The first-quarter earnings season has significantly outperformed expectations, with 83% of reporting S&P 500 companies beating analysts' estimates. First-quarter S&P 500 earnings are projected to climb nearly 29% year-over-year, marking a sixth consecutive quarter of double-digit earnings growth. The magnitude of these beats averaged 12.3%, well above the five-year average.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Poised to Maintain Interest Rates
items.summary: Investor sentiment suggests that the Federal Reserve is likely to keep interest rates unchanged through the end of 2026, with traders betting on the central bank holding rates in the 3.50% to 3.75% range. This outlook is supported by recent jobs data that, while strong, shows decelerating wage growth, easing immediate inflationary pressures.
